blockingIssues.description: The rerun fixes the old sidecar-shape collision, but `prepare_input_with_session` still seeds empty `{}` values with in-band sentinel JSON before invoking `session_input_callback`, so empty-object provenance still occupies callback-visible JSON value space and can leak if a callback mutates those seeded objects in place.

appliedUpdates.description: Recorded the session provenance invariant that `session_input_callback` must observe original public `InputItem` JSON values and that empty-object provenance bookkeeping must stay completely out of user-visible JSON value space.

rejectedObservations.summary: Accepted narrow omission for duplicate empty JSON object provenance inside session_input_callback.
rejectedObservations.justification: Full Python-style object-identity parity for duplicate empty JSON objects is not safely achievable under the current Rust by-value SessionInputCallback API without either an in-band marker or a public API change. The mission preserves the clean public API and will document this narrow omission explicitly in the final parity ledger refresh.
